title: grove project configuration
description: Machine-local grove.json (or grove.jsonc) that lives at a grove project root (beside the bare .base repo). Declares the recipes grove runs when a branch is opened, plus a few cross-recipe settings. The .jsonc variant (and either extension) tolerates // and /* */ comments and trailing commas.

$schema.description: Reference to this JSON Schema (for editor validation).

copy.description: Untracked files copied from the default-branch worktree into freshly created worktrees.

recipes.description: Ordered recipes run when a branch is opened.

detectSquash.description: Detect squash/rebase-merged branches via patch-equivalence against origin/<default> (default: true).

forge.description: Optionally consult a forge (via the gh CLI) for authoritative merged-PR state.

enabled.description: Enable the gh-based merged-PR lookup (default: false). Requires gh on PATH and authentication for the remote's host.

repo.description: Override the auto-detected host/owner/repo passed to `gh --repo` (e.g. github.com/owner/repo).

type.description: Recipe type. Built-ins: tmux, vscode-color-config, webhook, command. Any other value resolves to grove-recipe-<type> on PATH.

onCreate.description: Run when a worktree is freshly created (default: true).

onOpen.description: Run on every open - create, reopen, or plain-folder launch (default: true). Set false for one-time, create-only setup.

url.description: Target URL, e.g. http://127.0.0.1:39788/open via a reverse SSH tunnel.

token.description: Shared secret sent as 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'. Supports $VAR env substitution.

params.description: JSON body to POST. String values support $VAR / ${VAR} substitution from grove context env.

layout.description: tmux panes as name=cmd pairs, comma-separated (e.g. shell=,claude=claude).

command.description: Shell command run in the worktree through a login shell (e.g. nvm use && yarn install). Pair with "onOpen": false for one-time setup on new worktrees only.

shell.description: Login shell used to run the command (default: bash).
